question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted topic ID that resolves to a topic whose feeScheduleKey is null in the persisted Topic entity, causing the mirror node to report no fee schedule key exists, so downstream fee-exempt-key-list enforcement is bypassed and custom fees are permanently unchargeable, freezing collector accounts' expected revenue?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a lookup of a topic ID whose adminKey field is stored as an empty byte array (due to migration logic treating empty byte arrays as null) rather than null, causing the REST API to report a non-null adminKey when none exists, leading users to believe the topic is mutable when it is permanently immutable and funds locked in custom fee schedules cannot be updated?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user query a topic ID that exists in the topic table but whose feeScheduleKey was never persisted (null), causing FeeTopicStore.getTopic() to build a Topic object with custom fees but no fee schedule key, so no one can ever update the fee schedule and funds are permanently locked into an unmodifiable fee structure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that findById() returns a Topic with a non-null submitKey, and then craft a ConsensusSubmitMessage that is rejected because the mirror node's stale cached topic state shows a submitKey that has already been rotated on-chain, permanently preventing message submission and causing custom fee collector accounts to never receive expected fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() uses the raw numeric Long ID without shard/realm validation, allowing them to query a topic ID from a different shard that maps to the same numeric value, causing the fee calculation in FeeTopicStore to apply the wrong topic's custom fees and permanently drain funds from accounts submitting messages to the legitimate topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service on fee calculation by querying a topic ID that does not exist in the topic table, causing TopicServiceImpl.findById() to throw EntityNotFoundException, which if unhandled in fee calculation paths causes the entire transaction fee assessment to fail and funds to be permanently locked in pending state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition where findById() reads a Topic row mid-migration (during V1.103.2 backfill) that has a null feeExemptKeyList, causing the fee calculator to treat all submitters as non-exempt and charge custom fees to accounts that should be exempt, permanently draining their balances?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user submit messages to a topic whose Topic row in the database has a stale timestampRange (not yet updated after a ConsensusUpdateTopic transaction), causing findById() to return outdated feeExemptKeyList data, so a user whose key was added to the exempt list is still charged custom fees, permanently draining their token balance?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that FeeTopicStore.getTopic() calls findById(id.topicNum()) using only the topic number (ignoring shard and realm), causing a topic from shard 0 realm 0 to be confused with a topic from a different realm that has a different custom fee schedule, leading to incorrect fee charges that permanently drain user funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where findById() returns a Topic entity whose feeExemptKeyList is a serialized empty FeeExemptKeyList protobuf (non-null but semantically empty), but the fee calculator treats it as a populated list and exempts all submitters from fees, permanently preventing fee collectors from receiving their entitled custom fees and freezing their expected income?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that findById() exposes the raw adminKey bytes via the REST API response, allowing them to identify topics with no adminKey (null), and then submit a ConsensusUpdateTopic transaction that sets a feeScheduleKey to an attacker-controlled key, permanently locking the fee schedule under attacker control and freezing legitimate fee updates?
